title: कठोपनिषद् (प्रमुख श्लोक)
devanagariText:
॥ कठोपनिषद् — प्रमुख श्लोक ॥

ॐ सह नाववतु। सह नौ भुनक्तु।
सह वीर्यं करवावहै।
तेजस्वि नावधीतमस्तु। मा विद्विषावहै।
ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

श्रेयश्च प्रेयश्च मनुष्यमेतस्तौ
सम्परीत्य विविनक्ति धीरः।
श्रेयो हि धीरोऽभि प्रेयसो वृणीते
प्रेयो मन्दो योगक्षेमाद्वृणीते॥ १.२.२॥

न नरेणावरेण प्रोक्त एष
सुविज्ञेयो बहुधा चिन्त्यमानः।
अनन्यप्रोक्ते गतिरत्र नास्ति
अणीयान्ह्यतर्क्यमणुप्रमाणात्॥ १.२.८॥

उत्तिष्ठत जाग्रत प्राप्य वरान्निबोधत।
क्षुरस्य धारा निशिता दुरत्यया
दुर्गं पथस्तत्कवयो वदन्ति॥ १.३.१४॥

आत्मानँ रथिनं विद्धि शरीरँ रथमेव तु।
बुद्धिं तु सारथिं विद्धि मनः प्रग्रहमेव च॥ १.३.३॥

इन्द्रियाणि हयानाहुर्विषयांस्तेषु गोचरान्।
आत्मेन्द्रियमनोयुक्तं भोक्तेत्याहुर्मनीषिणः॥ १.३.४॥

यस्तु विज्ञानवान्भवति युक्तेन मनसा सदा।
तस्येन्द्रियाणि वश्यानि सदश्वा इव सारथेः॥ १.३.६॥

यस्त्वविज्ञानवान्भवत्यमनस्कः सदाऽशुचिः।
न स तत्पदमाप्नोति संसारं चाधिगच्छति॥ १.३.७॥

यस्तु विज्ञानवान्भवति समनस्कः सदा शुचिः।
स तु तत्पदमाप्नोति यस्माद्भूयो न जायते॥ १.३.८॥

इन्द्रियेभ्यः परा ह्यर्था अर्थेभ्यश्च परं मनः।
मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्बुद्धेरात्मा महान्परः॥ १.३.१०॥

महतः परमव्यक्तमव्यक्तात्पुरुषः परः।
पुरुषान्न परं किञ्चित्सा काष्ठा सा परा गतिः॥ १.३.११॥

एष सर्वेषु भूतेषु गूढोऽऽत्मा न प्रकाशते।
दृश्यते त्वग्र्यया बुद्ध्या सूक्ष्मया सूक्ष्मदर्शिभिः॥ १.३.१२॥

न जायते म्रियते वा विपश्चिन्
नायं कुतश्चिन्न बभूव कश्चित्।
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो
न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे॥ १.२.१८॥

अणोरणीयान्महतो महीयान्
आत्माऽस्य जन्तोर्निहितो गुहायाम्।
तमक्रतुः पश्यति वीतशोको
धातुप्रसादान्महिमानमात्मनः॥ १.२.२०॥

यदा सर्वे प्रभिद्यन्ते हृदयस्येह ग्रन्थयः।
अथ मर्त्योऽमृतो भवत्येतावद्ध्यनुशासनम्॥ २.३.१५॥

॥ इति कठोपनिषद् प्रमुख श्लोकाः सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Kathopanishad — Pramukh Shlok ||

Om Saha nav-avatu | Saha nau bhunaktu |
Saha viryam karavavhai |
Tejasvi nav-adhitam-astu | Ma vidvishavahai |
Om Shantih Shantih Shantih ||

Shreyash-cha preyash-cha manushyam-etas-tau
Samparitya vivinakti dhirah |
Shreyo hi dhiro-abhi preyaso vrinite
Preyo mando yoga-kshemad-vrinite || 1.2.2 ||

Na narena-avarena prokta esha
Suvijneyo bahudha chintyamanah |
Ananya-prokte gatir-atra nasti
Aniyaan-hy-atarkyam-anu-pramanat || 1.2.8 ||

Uttishthata jagrata prapya varan-nibodhata |
Kshurasya dhara nishita duratyaya
Durgam pathas-tat-kavayo vadanti || 1.3.14 ||

Atmanam rathinam viddhi shariram ratham-eva tu |
Buddhim tu sarathim viddhi manah pragraham-eva cha || 1.3.3 ||

Indriyani hayan-ahur-vishayams-teshu gocharan |
Atma-indriya-mano-yuktam bhokta-ity-ahur-manishinah || 1.3.4 ||

Yas-tu vijnanavaan-bhavati yuktena manasa sada |
Tasya-indriyani vashyani sad-ashva iva saratheh || 1.3.6 ||

Yas-tv-avijnanavaan-bhavaty-amanaskah sada-ashuchih |
Na sa tat-padam-apnoti samsaram cha-adhigachchhati || 1.3.7 ||

Yas-tu vijnanavaan-bhavati sa-manaskah sada shuchih |
Sa tu tat-padam-apnoti yasmad-bhuyo na jayate || 1.3.8 ||

Indriyebhyah para hy-artha arthebhyash-cha param manah |
Manasas-tu para buddhir-buddher-atma mahan-parah || 1.3.10 ||

Mahatah param-avyaktam-avyaktat-Purushah parah |
Purushat-na param kinchit-sa kashtha sa para gatih || 1.3.11 ||

Esha sarveshu bhuteshu gudho-atma na prakashate |
Drishyate tv-agryaya buddhya sukshmaya sukshma-darshibhih || 1.3.12 ||

Na jayate mriyate va vipashchin
Na-ayam kutash-chin-na babhuva kashchit |
Ajo nityah shashvato-ayam purano
Na hanyate hanyamane sharire || 1.2.18 ||

Anor-aniyaan-mahato mahiyaan
Atma-asya jantor-nihito guhayam |
Tam-akratuh pashyati vita-shoko
Dhatu-prasadan-mahimanam-atmanah || 1.2.20 ||

Yada sarve prabhidyante hridayasya-iha granthayah |
Atha martyo-amrito bhavaty-etavad-dhy-anushasanam || 2.3.15 ||

|| Iti Kathopanishad Pramukh Shlokah Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्रेय (कल्याण) और प्रेय (सुखकर) दोनों मनुष्य के पास आते हैं। धीर पुरुष उन्हें परखकर श्रेय को चुनता है; मूढ़ योगक्षेम के लिए प्रेय को चुनता है॥ १.२.२॥

किसी साधारण व्यक्ति द्वारा कहा गया यह आत्मतत्त्व सुविज्ञेय नहीं — बिना अनन्य (श्रेष्ठ) गुरु के यहाँ गति नहीं — क्योंकि यह अणु से भी सूक्ष्म, तर्कातीत है॥ १.२.८॥

उठो! जागो! श्रेष्ठ गुरुओं को प्राप्त कर ज्ञान प्राप्त करो। क्षुर (छुरे) की धार-सी तीक्ष्ण, दुर्गम यह मार्ग है — ऐसा कवि (विद्वान) कहते हैं॥ १.३.१४॥

आत्मा को रथी (स्वामी) जानो, शरीर को रथ, बुद्धि को सारथी, मन को लगाम। इन्द्रियाँ घोड़े हैं, विषय उनके मार्ग। आत्मा-इन्द्रिय-मन से युक्त जो है — उसे भोक्ता कहते हैं मनीषी॥ १.३.३-४॥

जो विज्ञानवान है, सदा संयत मन वाला — उसकी इन्द्रियाँ वश में हैं, जैसे अच्छे सारथी के अश्व। जो अविज्ञानवान, अमनस्क, सदा अशुचि — वह उस पद को नहीं पाता, संसार में पड़ता है। जो विज्ञानवान, सचेत, सदा शुचि — वह उस पद को पाता है जहाँ से फिर जन्म नहीं लेता॥ १.३.६-८॥

इन्द्रियों से परे अर्थ (विषय), अर्थों से परे मन, मन से परे बुद्धि, बुद्धि से परे महान आत्मा, महान से परे अव्यक्त, अव्यक्त से परे पुरुष — पुरुष से परे कुछ नहीं — वही काष्ठा (अन्तिम सीमा), वही परागति॥ १.३.१०-११॥

यह आत्मा न जन्मता, न मरता — अजन्मा, नित्य, शाश्वत, पुरातन — शरीर नष्ट होने पर भी नष्ट नहीं होता॥ १.२.१८॥

अणु से भी सूक्ष्म, महान से भी महान — आत्मा इस जीव की गुहा (हृदय) में छिपा है। निष्काम, शोकरहित पुरुष धातुप्रसाद (कृपा) से आत्मा की महिमा देखता है॥ १.२.२०॥

जब हृदय की सब ग्रन्थियाँ टूट जाती हैं — तब मर्त्य अमृत हो जाता है — इतना ही अनुशासन (शिक्षा) है॥ २.३.१५॥
benefits: Contains Uttishthata Jagrata — the immortal call to awaken
Ratha Kalpana — body as chariot, atma as charioteer metaphor
Teaches discrimination between Shreya (good) and Preya (pleasant)
Reveals Atman as unborn, eternal, and imperishable
Nachiketa's dialogue with Yama — supreme death-transcending wisdom
Invokes fearlessness and knowledge of immortality
description: Kathopanishad Key Verses — कठोपनिषद् (प्रमुख श्लोक) — is a sacred prayer offered to Lord Vishnu. Attributed to Kathopanishad (Krishna Yajurveda), this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to contain Uttishthata Jagrata — the immortal call to awaken as well as nachiketa' dialogue with Yama — supreme death-transcending wisdom. Additionally, it is known to grant fearlessness and knowledge of immortality. It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Guru Purnima for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 15 verses, it can be completed in approximately 12 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Kathopanishad Key Verses in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Kathopanishad key verses in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Nachiketa-Yama dialogue on Atman, immortality, and Uttishthata Jagrata.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Kathopanishad?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kathopanishad (कठोपनिषद्) — also called Katha Upanishad or Kathaka Upanishad — is one of the ten principal Upanishads (Mukhya Upanishads) recognized by all Vedanta traditions, drawn from the Katha Shakha (Katha branch) of the Krishna Yajurveda. Composed in two adhyayas of three vallis each (six chapters total, 119 verses), it is structured as a dialogue (samvada) between the boy-rishi Nachiketa (son of Sage Vajashravas Uddalaki) and Yama-raja, the king of death himself. After being sent to Yama by his angered father, Nachiketa receives three boons — peace for his father, knowledge of the heavenly fire-altar (Nachiketa-agni), and the ultimate boon: knowledge of what happens after death and the nature of the Atman. The Upanishad is celebrated for the Uttishthata Jagrata mantra, the chariot metaphor (Atmanam rathinam viddhi), and the immortal atman teaching (Na jayate mriyate va). (Source: Krishna Yajurveda — Kathaka Shakha.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Nachiketa-Yama dialogue and why is it important?
seoFaqs.answer: The Nachiketa-Yama samvada is the central narrative of the Kathopanishad. Young Nachiketa, sent angrily by his father Vajashravas to the abode of Yama (death), waited three days at Yama's gates without food. To compensate, Yama granted three boons: (1) Peace and recognition for father Vajashravas; (2) Knowledge of the special fire-ritual (Nachiketa-agni) that grants svarga-loka; (3) The ultimate question — does the Atman survive death? Yama tried to dissuade Nachiketa from the third boon by offering wealth, kingdoms, beautiful celestial maidens, sons and grandsons living a hundred years, and even Yama's own kingdom — but Nachiketa rejected all (Shvo-bhava martyasya yad-antakaitat — these are all transient and consume the senses anyway), insisting on the boon of atman-jnana. Pleased, Yama then taught the supreme Brahman-vidya — the immortal Atman, the chariot metaphor, the Aum upasana, and the path beyond death. (Sources: Kathopanishad Adhyaya 1, Vallis 1-3.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous Uttishthata Jagrata mantra and its meaning?
seoFaqs.answer: The mantra (1.3.14) — Uttishthata jagrata prapya varan-nibodhata, kshurasya dhara nishita duratyaya, durgam pathas-tat-kavayo vadanti — is one of the most quoted verses in the entire Upanishadic corpus: Arise (Uttishthata)! Awake (Jagrata)! Approach the wise (varan — the realized teachers) and learn (nibodhata)! The path is sharp as the razor's edge (kshurasya dhara nishita), difficult to traverse (duratyaya), arduous (durgam) — so the wise ones (kavayah) declare. This mantra was famously chosen by Swami Vivekananda as his life-motto and the rallying-call of his entire Vedanta-renaissance — it adorns the entry-arch of Belur Math, was inscribed on his lectern at the Chicago Parliament of Religions (1893), and remains the foundational call to spiritual awakening for all Hindu-renaissance movements (Ramakrishna Mission, Aurobindo Ashram, Chinmaya Mission). (Sources: Kathopanishad 1.3.14; Vivekananda's Lectures from Colombo to Almora.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the chariot metaphor (Ratha Kalpana) of the Kathopanishad?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 1.3.3-9 contain the famous Ratha Kalpana (chariot allegory) — the foundational metaphor of Indian psychology used by every later Vedanta and Yoga teacher: Atmanam rathinam viddhi shariram ratham-eva tu, buddhim tu sarathim viddhi manah pragraham-eva cha, indriyani hayan-ahur-vishayams-teshu gocharan, atma-indriya-mano-yuktam bhokta-ity-ahur-manishinah — Know the Atman as the rathi (chariot-rider, owner), the body as the ratha (chariot), the buddhi (intellect) as the sarathi (charioteer), the manas (mind) as the pragraha (reins), the indriyas (sense-organs) as the hayah (horses), the vishayas (sense-objects) as their gochara (pasture-paths). The Atman together with senses-mind is called the bhokta (enjoyer) by the wise. The same metaphor appears in Plato's Phaedrus (~370 BCE) — establishing parallel Greek-Indian philosophical thought. The verses then develop the metaphor: well-yoked horses (controlled senses) lead to the destination (Vishnu's pada / moksha); unyoked horses lead to samsara. (Sources: Kathopanishad 1.3.3-9; classical Indian psychology.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the Kathopanishad teach about the Atman and immortality?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kathopanishad's central teaching on Atman is given in verses 1.2.18-20: (1) Na jayate mriyate va vipashchin, na-ayam kutash-chin-na babhuva kashchit, ajo nityah shashvato-ayam purano, na hanyate hanyamane sharire — The wise Atman is not born and does not die; it does not arise from anywhere nor become anything; unborn (aja), eternal (nitya), permanent (shashvata), ancient (purana) — it is not slain when the body is slain. This verse appears verbatim in Bhagavad Gita 2.20 — Krishna directly quoting the Kathopanishad to Arjuna on the Kurukshetra battlefield. (2) Anor-aniyaan-mahato mahiyaan-atma-asya jantor-nihito guhayam — The Atman, smaller than the smallest and greater than the greatest, dwells in the cave (guha) of the heart of every being. The Atman is realized only by the akratu (desireless) and vita-shoka (sorrow-free) through the dhatu-prasada (grace-of-the-elements / divine grace). (Sources: Kathopanishad 1.2.18-20; Bhagavad Gita 2.20.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Kathopanishad be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kathopanishad is most powerfully recited on Thursday (Guruvar — the day of Brihaspati and Vishnu, ideal for wisdom-Upanishads), Saturday (Shanivar — the day of Yama-Shani and ancestor-veneration, fitting for the death-dialogue), Guru Purnima / Vyasa Purnima (Ashadha Purnima — the day of guru-veneration), Dipavali Yama-Tarpana day (Naraka Chaturdashi — when offerings to Yama are made), Rishi Panchami (Bhadrapad Shukla Panchami — the day of the seven seers), as a daily Upanishad-svadhyaya, before any major life-decision (where the Shreya-Preya discrimination of 1.2.2 is invoked), as a death-bed reading (the Atman's immortality verses bring fearlessness to the dying), and at any major shoka (grief) or fear-of-death situation. Adi Shankaracharya's 9th-century bhashya is the standard commentary. The full recitation takes about 1 hour; key verses about 12 minutes. (Sources: Vedanta svadhyaya tradition; Upanishad parayana paddhati.)
